ranked_movies.title: Good Will Hunting
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in the 'human moments' request for its beautiful writing, themes of growth and healing, and powerful performances.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested for its poetic, quiet exploration of human connection, loneliness, and beautifully real moments.

ranked_movies.title: Marriage Story
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its raw, honest, and heartbreaking portrayal of a relationship's breakdown and its deeply human moments.

ranked_movies.title: The Station Agent
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation for a movie with 'beautifully written human moments,' specifically praised for its portrayal of grief, awkwardness, and connection.

ranked_movies.title: When Harry Met Sally
ranked_movies.reason: The top recommendation for a cozy, feel-good movie similar to 'You've Got Mail,' praised for its charming romance and witty dialogue.

ranked_movies.title: Sicario
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a highly-upvoted post praising its efficient script, masterful tension, and standout performances, particularly Josh Brolin's.

ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a top-tier feel-good romance and a favorite rom-com, hitting the desired warm and fuzzy vibe.

ranked_movies.title: Iron Man
ranked_movies.reason: The most upvoted recommendation for a superhero movie worth watching for non-fans, praised as being 'really, really good.'

ranked_movies.title: Paris, Texas
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its poetic and human storytelling, fitting the request for films about grief, love, and healing.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a deep, highly-upvoted analytical discussion on r/TrueFilm about Jack Torrance's character and the film's themes.

ranked_movies.title: The Dark Knight
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a superhero movie that transcends the genre and is worth watching for anyone.

ranked_movies.title: Green Room
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a highly-upvoted post praising its execution and gnarly, realistic death scenes that left a strong impression.

ranked_movies.title: Shutter Island
ranked_movies.reason: Central to a discussion about Martin Scorsese's work, with strong opinions on both sides regarding its quality and place in his filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Notorious
ranked_movies.reason: A highly recommended 'lesser-watched' Hitchcock film, with one commenter naming it their personal favorite.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: A primary example discussed in the request for 'hateful, mean-spirited, nihilistic movies,' noted for making viewers feel guilty for laughing.

ranked_movies.title: Strangers on a Train
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an 'absolutely excellent' and must-watch Alfred Hitchcock film that is slightly less famous than his top-tier works.

ranked_movies.title: Sinister
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a popular post discussing its effectiveness as a horror film, with many comments sharing how it induced extreme anxiety.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated post seeking it out, highlighting its high demand and status as a modern classic in the zombie genre.

ranked_movies.title: American History X
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for having one of the most brutal and memorable death scenes in film history, specifically the curb stomp sequence.

ranked_movies.title: The Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended alongside other cozy, feel-good rom-coms for its charming premise and ability to create a warm, fuzzy feeling.
